items.front: 6-2-1: What divergence angle is required to apply diverging-course initial separation minima?
items.back: 45 degrees or more. Aircraft must depart the same or adjacent airports.
1. Immediately after takeoff → 1 minute. 2. Within 5 minutes after takeoff → 2 minutes. 3. Within 13 miles DME/ATD after takeoff → 3 miles. All measured until courses diverge.

items.front: 6-2-1 note 2: How are helipad/vertipad departures referenced for diverging-course minima?
items.back: Takeoff course = runway centerline reference. Helipad/vertipad center = threshold.

items.front: 6-2-1b: Terminal — parallel runways same-direction simultaneous takeoffs with diverging courses
items.back: Authorize when centerlines are parallel, separated by at least 3,500 feet, and aircraft fly diverging courses immediately after takeoff.

items.front: 6-2-1c1: Terminal — nonintersecting runways divergence and centerline distance table
items.back: 30°+ → no distance req beyond nonintersecting. 15-29° → ≥2,000 ft. <15° → ≥3,500 ft.

items.front: 6-2-1c2: Terminal — intersecting runways: when is a turn required before succeeding takeoff?
items.back: When divergence is 15-29°: preceding aircraft must have passed intersection AND commenced a turn. For 30°+: only intersection passage required, no turn needed.

items.front: 6-2-1c: What is the key difference between nonintersecting and intersecting diverging runway rules?
items.back: Nonintersecting uses centerline distance thresholds. Intersecting uses intersection-passage + optional turn requirement (15-29° only). Centerline distance does not apply to intersecting.
3 minutes until following aircraft passes through leading aircraft's assigned altitude; OR 5 miles between eligible DME/ATD aircraft.

items.front: 6-2-2: When is mixed DME/ATD 5-mile minimum authorized?
items.back: DME aircraft must be EITHER at/below 10,000 feet OR outside 10 miles from DME NAVAID. Both conditions need not be met — either one suffices.

items.front: 6-2-2: Which same-course departure pairs are unconditionally eligible for 5 miles?
items.back: DME-to-DME and ATD-to-ATD pairs. Only mixed DME/ATD pairs have the altitude/distance restriction.

items.front: 6-2-2: What triggers the same-course departure minimums to apply?
items.back: The following aircraft will climb through the altitude assigned to the leading aircraft. If no climb-through occurs, 6-2-2 does not govern the separation.
